body:
## Problem
`/lists` is the hub page for all 98 Top-10 drummer list pages (sitemap priority 0.9, linked from nav). Its meta handler in `/home/runner/work/MetalDrummerGear/MetalDrummerGear/api/meta/[...path].js:2580-2592` returns `title`/`description`/`image`/`ssrLinks` only — **no `articleSchema` key at all**. Verified by direct read:

```js
if (path === '/lists') {
  return {
    title: `Top 10 Metal Drummer Lists | ${SITE_NAME}`,
    description: 'Ranked lists of the best metal drummers by speed, technique, and genre. Fastest blast beats, best double bass, most innovative and more.',
    image: DEFAULT_IMAGE,
    type: 'website',
    url: `${BASE_URL}/lists`,
    ssrLinks: Object.values(TOP_10_LISTS).map(list => ({
      href: `/lists/${list.slug}`,
      label: list.title,
    })),
  };
}
```

Sibling hubs (`/drummers`, `/genres`, `/battles`, `/bands`) all emit a `CollectionPage` (or `@graph`-wrapped) `articleSchema`. `/lists` is the outlier. This is distinct from #4355 (which only added `ssrLinks` to the 9 hubs, no schema) and from #1083 (closed — that covered `/lists/<slug>` individual pages, not the `/lists` hub itself).

## Fix
In `/home/runner/work/MetalDrummerGear/MetalDrummerGear/api/meta/[...path].js`, add an `articleSchema` field to the `/lists` handler (~line 2580), following the same `CollectionPage` pattern used by the `/genres` handler (search for `path === '/genres'` in the same file for the exact shape). Example:

```js
articleSchema: JSON.stringify({
  '@context': 'https://schema.org',
  '@type': 'CollectionPage',
  name: 'Top 10 Metal Drummer Lists',
  url: `${BASE_URL}/lists`,
  description: 'Ranked lists of the best metal drummers by speed, technique, and genre.',
  publisher: { '@type': 'Organization', name: 'MetalForge', url: BASE_URL },
  hasPart: Object.values(TOP_10_LISTS).map(list => ({
    '@type': 'ItemList',
    name: list.title,
    url: `${BASE_URL}/lists/${list.slug}`,
  })),
}),
```

Reference: https://schema.org/CollectionPage

## Verify
1. `curl -s https://metalforge.io/lists -A Googlebot | grep -A2 'application/ld+json'` — confirm a `CollectionPage` JSON-LD block is now present.
2. Validate the emitted JSON with `node -e "JSON.parse(...)"` or https://validator.schema.org/ — no parse errors.
3. Confirm the block includes all 98 list entries (or a reasonable `hasPart` summary) — count should match `Object.keys(TOP_10_LISTS).length`.
4. Confirm no regression to the existing `ssrLinks` output (still renders the `<ul>` of 98 `<li>` links from #4355).
5. Confirm sibling hubs (`/genres`, `/drummers`) are unaffected (no shared code path broken).

## Done
`/lists` emits a valid `CollectionPage` JSON-LD block alongside its existing `ssrLinks`, matching the schema pattern already used on sibling hub pages.
title: SEO: Add CollectionPage schema to /lists hub page (currently zero JSON-LD)
